content.body: DIRECT CURRENT (DC): Current flows in one direction only. From batteries. Constant voltage.
ALTERNATING CURRENT (AC): Current reverses direction periodically. From generators and mains supply. In Uganda/UK: 50Hz frequency (50 cycles per second). 240V mains.

Frequency (f) = 1/Period (T) → f in Hz, T in seconds
Peak voltage (V₀) = √2 × RMS voltage
RMS voltage = Peak voltage ÷ √2 ≈ 0.707 × V₀

TRANSFORMER: Uses electromagnetic induction to change AC voltage.
Step-up transformer: increases voltage (more secondary coils)
Step-down transformer: decreases voltage (fewer secondary coils)

Transformer equation: Vp/Vs = Np/Ns
For ideal transformer: Vp × Ip = Vs × Is (power conservation)
where p = primary, s = secondary, V = voltage, N = turns, I = current

content.title: Transformer Calculation
content.body:
Primary: 240V, 1000 turns. Secondary: 12V.
Ns = Np × (Vs/Vp) = 1000 × (12/240) = 50 turns
If primary current = 0.1A: Is = Vp×Ip/Vs = 240×0.1/12 = 2A

questions.question: AC mains in Uganda has frequency:
questions.explanation: Uganda (and most of Africa/Europe) uses 50 Hz AC mains — 50 complete cycles per second.

questions.question: RMS voltage is the ___ of AC that has the same heating effect as the same value of DC.
questions.explanation: RMS (Root Mean Square) is the effective AC voltage — it equals the DC value with same power output.

questions.question: Vp/Vs = Np/Ns. If Np = 500, Ns = 100, Vp = 200V, then Vs =
questions.explanation: Vs = Vp × (Ns/Np) = 200 × (100/500) = 40V. Step-down transformer.

questions.question: A step-up transformer:
questions.explanation: Power is conserved: V×I = constant. More V → less I. Step-up transformer increases V, decreases I.

questions.question: Why is electricity transmitted at high voltage over long distances?
options: Less current needed → less energy lost as heat in wires
Low voltage is not allowed in cables
questions.answer: Less current needed → less energy lost as heat in wires
questions.explanation: Power loss = I²R. Less current at high V → much less power wasted as heat in transmission cables.

questions.question: Transformers only work with:
questions.explanation: Transformers need changing magnetic flux (from AC) to induce EMF. DC produces constant flux — no induction.

questions.question: Peak voltage of 240V RMS mains supply is:
Period T of 50 Hz AC =
questions.explanation: T = 1/f = 1/50 = 0.02 seconds

questions.question: An ideal transformer has Np = 2000, Ns = 100, Is = 10A. Primary current Ip =
questions.explanation: Vp×Ip = Vs×Is. Also Np/Ns = Vs/Vp → Ip/Is = Ns/Np. Ip = 10×(100/2000) = 0.5A

questions.question: Electricity from power station goes through step-up transformer before transmission because:
options: To reduce current and minimise power loss in cables
To make it safer for homes
questions.answer: To reduce current and minimise power loss in cables
questions.explanation: Higher voltage → lower current → P_loss = I²R is minimised in long-distance cables.

questions.question: Before entering homes, electricity passes through a step-___ transformer.
questions.explanation: High transmission voltage (e.g. 33,000V) is stepped down to 240V (mains) for safe home use.

questions.question: The core of a transformer is made of laminated iron to:
questions.explanation: Thin laminated (layered) iron reduces eddy currents that would otherwise waste energy as heat.

questions.question: If transformer efficiency is 100%, power input equals:
questions.explanation: 100% efficiency: all input power converts to output power. Vp×Ip = Vs×Is.

questions.question: An oscilloscope shows AC as:
questions.explanation: AC voltage varies sinusoidally with time — an oscilloscope displays it as a smooth sine wave.

questions.question: Np = 100 turns, Ns = 2000 turns. This is a:
questions.explanation: More secondary turns than primary → Vs > Vp → step-up (increases voltage).

questions.question: The frequency of AC from a generator depends on:
options: Speed of rotation of the generator
questions.answer: Speed of rotation of the generator
questions.explanation: Faster rotation = more cycles per second = higher frequency.

questions.question: A 12V, 3A device connected to 240V mains needs a transformer with turns ratio (Np:Ns) of:
questions.explanation: Vp/Vs = Np/Ns. 240/12 = 20. So Np:Ns = 20:1 (step-down).

questions.question: Which device converts AC to DC?
questions.explanation: A rectifier (using diodes) converts AC to pulsating DC. Used in phone chargers and power supplies.

questions.question: The power loss in a transmission cable with resistance R carrying current I is:
questions.explanation: Power = I²R. This is why reducing current (by increasing voltage) dramatically reduces power loss in cables.

questions.question: DC from batteries is used in preference to AC for:
questions.explanation: Electronic circuits (phones, laptops) need stable DC. Batteries provide portable, safe DC power.
